popularStory.headline: en.Wikipedia Eleanor of Provence
popularStory.description: Eleanor of Provence (c. 1223 – 24/25 June 1291) was a French noblewoman who became Queen consort of England as the wife of King Henry III from 1236 until his death in 1272. She served as regent of Eng …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Eleanor Berenger of Provence Queen of England於1221年1月24日出生在法國布-度-o艾--普洛福斯時，她的父親Count Raimond Berenger de Provence IV25歲，母親Béatrice de Savoie22歲。她於1236年1月14日與Henry III King of England在英國肯特坎特布瑞坎特布瑞 凱德拉爾結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她於1253年被列為keeper of the great seal。她去世於1291年6月24日在英國英格蘭維爾爾阿姆斯波利享年70歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭維爾爾阿姆斯波利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Eleanor Berenger of Provence Queen of England于1221年1月24日出生在法国布-度-o艾--普洛福斯时，她的父亲Count Raimond Berenger de Provence IV25岁，母亲Béatrice de Savoie22岁。她于1236年1月14日与Henry III King of England在英国肯特坎特布瑞坎特布瑞 凱德拉爾结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她于1253年被列为keeper of the great seal。她去世于1291年6月24日在英国英格兰維爾爾阿姆斯波利享年70岁，埋葬在英国英格兰維爾爾阿姆斯波利。

nameMeanings.name: Queen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: shortened form of   McQueen  .

English: from the Middle English female personal name Quene, sometimes a pet form of Quenill (Old English Cwēnhild, from cwēn ‘queen’ + hild ‘battle’). Early examples of the name are mainly from southern England, especially Sussex, but its currency in late medieval northern England has also been suggested by records from Yorkshire.
English: possibly a nickname from Middle English quene ‘woman; harlot; crone’ (Old English cwēn), but this is less likely than 2 to have given rise to a hereditary surname.

variants.name: McQueen
meanings: Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Shuibhne ‘son of Suibhne’ a byname meaning ‘pleasant’. Compare McSweeney .

Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Shuain, a Gaelic patronymic based on Old Norse sveinn ‘boy, servant’, which was also used as a personal name (see Swain ). This is given as the origin of the McQueen or McSwan family of Skye, although the spellings are hard to distinguish. Compare Whan .
Americanized form of French   Moquin  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: England
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Middle English Engelond ‘England’. It was probably a formal alternative to English , which is also well evidenced as a surname in England. These names may have been acquired by English landowners who moved in Norman social circles or who lived in a neighbouring country (Scotland, Wales, or Ireland), or by English merchants who traded abroad.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of various farmsteads so named, from Old Norse eng ‘meadow’ + land ‘land’.
Swedish: ornamental name with the same meaning as 2.

variants.name: English
meanings: English, Scottish, and Welsh: ethnic name from Middle English English, Inglish, Inglis ‘English’ (Old English Englisc), sometimes alternating with Anglo-Norman French Engleis, Engles, Anglais, Angles (Old French Englois). Compare Inglis and England . Among the aristocracy and upper gentry it marked out a man of English ancestry from one of Norman or continental origin. In counties bordering England with Scotland and Wales the name distinguished an Englishman from a Scot or a native Welshman on both sides of the border. The name may also have been acquired by English merchants who traded abroad or who lived and worked in a ‘French’ borough in England (one exclusively administered by Normans).
Irish: in Ireland, this name was used to denote an Englishman, often being adopted for Irish Aingléis ‘Englishman’ or through mistranslation for Mac an Ghallóglaigh, see Gallogly and Golightly .

nameMeanings.name: Eleanor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

variants.name: Ellenor
meanings: Variant spelling of   Eleanor  , the result of blending with   Ellen .

variants.name: Elenor
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

variants.name: Elinor
meanings: Variant spelling of   Eleanor .

nameMeanings.name: Berenger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French (Bérenger): variant of Béringer (see Beringer ).
English (of Norman origin): variant of   Berringer  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Beringer
meanings: German; French (Béringer): from the ancient Germanic personal name Beringari, Beringer, composed of the elements bern ‘bear’ + ger ‘spear’. Compare French Belanger and Berenger .
German: habitational name for someone from any of several paces called Behringen or Böhringen in Baden-Württemberg and Bayern. Compare   Baringer  ,   Behringer  ,   Berringer  , and   Boehringer  .
English (of Norman origin): variant of   Berringer  .

variants.name: Berenguer
meanings: Spanish and French (southern; Bérenguer): from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements ber ‘bear’ + ger ‘spear’, a cognate of French Bérenger (see Berenger ) and Béringer (see Beringer ).

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Oswald or any of the various other names beginning with Os-.

nameMeanings.name: Provence
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Province   1, a cognate of 3 below. This surname is very rare in Britain.
Probably also an altered form of   Provance   1 and hence a variant of   Purviance  , a surname of (ultimately) Scottish origin. Compare   Provance   1.
In some cases possibly also French: habitational name from Provence, a region in southern France (see   Provencal  ). Compare   Province   3 and also 1 above.

variants.name: Province
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Provence, a region in southern France (see   Provencal  ). Compare   Provence   1 and   Provins   1.
Altered form of   Provance   1 and hence a variant of   Purviance  , a surname of (ultimately) Scottish origin. Compare   Provins   2.
Americanized form of French   Provence   3.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Eleanor Berenger of Provence Queen of England was born on 24 January 1221, in Aix-en-Provence, Bouches-du-Rhône, France, her father, Count Raimond Berenger de Provence IV, was 25 and her mother, Béatrice de Savoie, was 22. She married Henry III King of England on 14 January 1236, in Canterbury Cathedral, Canterbury, Kent, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 3 daughters. In 1253, her occupation is listed as keeper of the great seal. She died on 24 June 1291, in Amesbury, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 70, and was buried in Amesbury, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Eleanor Berenger of Provence Queen of England est née le 24 janvier 1221, à Aix-en-Provence, Bouches-du-Rhône, France, son père, Count Raimond Berenger de Provence IV, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Béatrice de Savoie, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé Henry III King of England le 14 janvier 1236, à Canterbury Cathedral, Canterbury, Kent, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 3 filles. En 1253, la profession de sa indiquée est keeper of the great seal. Elle est décédée le 24 juin 1291, à Amesbury, Wiltshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 70 ans, et a été enterrée à Amesbury, Wiltshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Eleanor Berenger of Provence Queen of England am 24. Januar 1221 in Aix-en-Provence, Bouches-du-Rhône, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Count Raimond Berenger de Provence IV 25 und ihre Mutter Béatrice de Savoie 22. Sie hat am 14. Januar 1236 in Canterbury Cathedral, Canterbury, Kent, England Henry III King of England geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 3 Töchter. 1253 ist ihr Beruf als keeper of the great seal aufgeführt. Sie starb am 24. Juni 1291 im Alter von 70 Jahren in Amesbury, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Amesbury, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Eleanor Berenger of Provence Queen of England è nata il il 24 gennaio 1221 nel Aix-en-Provence, Bouches-du-Rhône, Francia, sua padre, Count Raimond Berenger de Provence IV, aveva 25 anni e sua madre, Béatrice de Savoie, aveva 22 anni. Lei sposato Henry III King of England il 14 gennaio 1236, nel Canterbury Cathedral, Canterbury, Kent, England. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 3 figlie. ja.lifeSummary: Eleanor Berenger of Provence Queen of Englandが1221年1月24日にフランスボウチェス-ドウ-ルhoンエイクス-エン-プローベンスで生まれた時，父Count Raimond Berenger de Provence IVは25歳，母Béatrice de Savoieは22歳であった。1236年1月14日にイギリスケントカンテルブリーカンテルブリー カスエドラルでHenry III King of Englandと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。1253年，職業はkeeper of the great sealと記録されている。彼女は1291年6月24日にイギリスイギリスウィルトシュアイアエイメスブリーで70歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスウィルトシュアイアエイメスブリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Eleanor Berenger of Provence Queen of England(이)가 1221년 1월 24일 프랑스 부체스-드-ho니 아이스--플로븐스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Count Raimond Berenger de Provence IV(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Béatrice de Savoie(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 1236년 1월 14일 영국 켄트 캉르버이 캉르버이 캐테드럴에서 Henry III King of England(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 1253년, 그녀의 직업은 keeper of the great seal이었다. 그녀는 1291년 6월 24일 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르 아메스버이에서 70세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르 아메스버이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Eleanor Berenger of Provence Queen of England nasceu em 24 de janeiro de 1221, em Aix-en-Provence, Bouches-du-Rhône, França, seu pai, Count Raimond Berenger de Provence IV, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Béatrice de Savoie, tinha 22 anos. Ela casou-se com Henry III King of England em 14 de janeiro de 1236, em Canterbury Cathedral, Canterbury, Kent, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 3 filhas. Em 1253, sua profissão aparece como keeper of the great seal. Ela faleceu em 24 de junho de 1291, em Amesbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 70 anos, e foi sepultada em Amesbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Eleanor Berenger of Provence Queen of England родилась 24 января 1221 г. в местности эикс-ен-провенс, боучес-ду-рoн, Франция, её отцу по имени Count Raimond Berenger de Provence IV было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Béatrice de Savoie, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Henry III King of England 14 января 1236 г. в местности кантирбури кэтедраль, кантирбури, кэнт, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 3 дочери. В 1253 году она работала по профессии keeper of the great seal. Она умерла 24 июня 1291 г. в местности амесбури, уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 70 лет и была похоронена в местности амесбури, уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания.

Se casó con Henry III King of England el 14 de enero de 1236, en Canterbury Cathedral, Canterbury, Kent, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 24 de junio de 1291, en Amesbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 70 años, y fue sepultada en Amesbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
